<content>1: # Implementation Plan for AR-012 through AR-022
2: 
3: ## Overview
4: This document provides a detailed implementation plan for Architectural Requirements AR-012 through AR-022, covering CI/CD, CLI Toolkit, Code Execution, Deployment, Idle Cleanup, Integrations, Python SDK, Sandbox Orchestration, Session Routing, Session Store, and Project Infrastructure components.
5: 
6: **CRITICAL REQUIREMENT:**
7: - ALL code MUST be production-ready
8: - NO placeholders like "// TODO implementation here"
9: - NO empty functions or stubs
10: - EVERY Architectural Requirement MUST result in physical file changes
11: - Failure to write complete code will result in test failure
12: 
13: ---
14: 
15: ## AR-012: CI/CD Pipeline
16: 
17: ### Purpose
18: Automate validation on pull requests (build, lint, tests, codegen drift checks), publish container images on pushes to main and tags, run E2E tests on Kind, exercise the Python SDK, and enforce spelling and copyright consistency.
19:

430: ```yaml
431: name: Approve Workflows
432: on:
433:   pull_request_target:
434:     types: [labeled, synchronize]
435:     branches: [main, release-**]
436: 
437: jobs:
438:   approve:
439:     runs-on: ubuntu-latest
440:     steps:
441:       - uses: actions/checkout@v3
442:         with:
443:           ref: ${{ github.event.pull_request.head.sha }}
444:       
445:       - name: Approve workflows
446:         uses: actions/github-script@v6
447:         with:
448:           script: |
449:             const pr = context.payload.pull_request;
450:             const contributor = pr.user.login;
451:             
452:             const prs = await github.rest.search.issuesAndPullRequests({
453:               q: `is:pr author:${contributor} repo:${context.repo.owner}/${context.repo.repo}`
454:             });
455:             
456:             const isFirstTime = prs.data.total_count === 1;
457:             const hasOkToTest = pr.labels.some(label => label.name === 'ok-to-test');
458:             
459:             if (!isFirstTime || hasOkToTest) {
460:               const runs = await github.rest.actions.listWorkflowRunsForRepo({
461:                 owner: context.repo.owner,
462:                 repo: context.repo.repo,
463:                 head_sha: context.payload.pull_request.head.sha,
464:                 status: 'action_required'
465:               });
466:               
467:               for (const run of runs.data.workflow_runs) {
468:                 await github.rest.actions.approveWorkflowRun({
469:                   owner: context.repo.owner,
470:                   repo: context.repo.repo,
471:                   run_id: run.id
472:                 });
473:               }
474:             }
475: ```

593:     kubectl -n "$AGENTCUBE_NAMESPACE" rollout status deployment/redis --timeout=120s
594:     
595:     # Install AgentCube chart
596:     echo "Installing AgentCube Helm chart..."
597:     helm upgrade --install agentcube manifests/charts/base \
598:         --namespace "$AGENTCUBE_NAMESPACE" --create-namespace \
599:         --set redis.addr="redis.${AGENTCUBE_NAMESPACE}.svc.cluster.local:6379" \
600:         --set redis.password="" \
601:         --set workloadmanager.image.repository=workloadmanager \
602:         --set workloadmanager.image.tag=latest \
603:         --set-json 'workloadmanager.extraEnv=[{"name":"REDIS_PASSWORD_REQUIRED","value":"false"},{"name":"JWT_KEY_SECRET_NAMESPACE","value":"'$AGENTCUBE_NAMESPACE'"}]' \
604:         --set router.image.repository=agentcube-router \
605:         --set router.image.tag=latest \
606:         --set router.rbac.create=true \
607:         --set router.serviceAccountName=agentcube-router \
608:         --set-json 'router.extraEnv=[{"name":"REDIS_PASSWORD_REQUIRED","value":"false"}]' \
609:         --wait
610:     
611:     # Apply test fixtures
612:     echo "Applying test fixtures..."
613:     kubectl apply -f test/e2e/echo_agent.yaml
614:     kubectl apply -f test/e2e/e2e_code_interpreter.yaml
615:     
616:     # Port forward
617:     echo "Setting up port forwards..."
618:     kubectl port-forward -n "$AGENTCUBE_NAMESPACE" svc/workloadmanager "$WORKLOAD_MANAGER_LOCAL_PORT":8080 > /dev/null 2>&1 &
619:     WORKLOAD_MANAGER_PID=$!
620:     kubectl port-forward -n "$AGENTCUBE_NAMESPACE" svc/agentcube-router "$ROUTER_LOCAL_PORT":8080 > /dev/null 2>&1 &
621:     ROUTER_PID=$!
622:     
623:     # Wait for services to be ready
624:     echo "Waiting for services to be ready..."
625:     local max_attempts=60
626:     local attempt=0
627:     while [ $attempt -lt $max_attempts ]; do
628:         if curl -s http://localhost:"$WORKLOAD_MANAGER_LOCAL_PORT"/health > /dev/null 2>&1 && \
629:            curl -s http://localhost:"$ROUTER_LOCAL_PORT"/health/live > /dev/null 2>&1; then
630:             echo "Services are ready"
631:             break
632:         fi
633:         attempt=$((attempt + 1))
634:         sleep 2
635:     done
636:     
637:     if [ $attempt -eq $max_attempts ]; then
638:         echo "Failed to wait for services"
639:         kill $WORKLOAD_MANAGER_PID $ROUTER_PID || true
640:         exit 1
641:     fi

643:     # Create test service account and get token

650:     API_TOKEN=$(kubectl create token e2e-test -n "$AGENTCUBE_NAMESPACE" --duration=24h)
651:     export API_TOKEN

654: # Setup Python venv
655: echo "Setting up Python venv..."

782: ```python
783: #!/usr/bin/env python3
784: import os
785: import sys
786: import unittest
787: from agentcube import CodeInterpreterClient
788: 
789: WORKLOAD_MANAGER_URL = os.getenv("WORKLOAD_MANAGER_URL", "http://localhost:8080")
790: ROUTER_URL = os.getenv("ROUTER_URL", "http://localhost:8081")
791: API_TOKEN = os.getenv("API_TOKEN", "")
792: AGENTCUBE_NAMESPACE = os.getenv("AGENTCUBE_NAMESPACE", "agentcube")
793: 
794: 
795: class TestCodeInterpreterE2E(unittest.TestCase):
796:     """End-to-end tests for CodeInterpreter SDK"""
797: 
798:     def setUp(self):
799:         self.client = CodeInterpreterClient(
800:             name="e2e-test-interpreter",
801:             namespace=AGENTCUBE_NAMESPACE,
802:             workload_manager_url=WORKLOAD_MANAGER_URL,
803:             router_url=ROUTER_URL,
804:             auth_token=API_TOKEN if API_TOKEN else None,
805:             verbose=True,
806:         )
807: 
808:     def tearDown(self):
809:         self.client.stop()
810: 
811:     def test_session_creation(self):
812:         """Test that a session can be created"""
813:         self.assertIsNotNone(self.client.session_id)
814:         self.assertTrue(len(self.client.session_id) > 0)
815: 
816:     def test_execute_command(self):
817:         """Test executing a simple command"""
818:         output = self.client.execute_command("echo 'hello world'")
819:         self.assertIn("hello world", output)
820: 
821:     def test_run_python_code(self):
822:         """Test running Python code"""
823:         code = """
824: result = 2 + 2
825: print(f"2 + 2 = {result}")
826: """
827:         output = self.client.run_code(language="python", code=code)
828:         self.assertIn("2 + 2 = 4", output)
829: 
830:     def test_write_and_read_file(self):
831:         """Test writing and reading a file"""
832:         self.client.write_file(content="test content", remote_path="test.txt")
833:         files = self.client.list_files(path=".")
834:         self.assertTrue(any(f["name"] == "test.txt" for f in files["files"]))
835: 
836:     def test_session_cleanup(self):
837:         """Test that session is properly cleaned up"""
838:         session_id = self.client.session_id
839:         self.client.stop()
840:         
841:         # Try to use the same session should fail
842:         with self.assertRaises(Exception):
843:             client = CodeInterpreterClient(
844:                 name="e2e-test-interpreter",
845:                 namespace=AGENTCUBE_NAMESPACE,
846:                 workload_manager_url=WORKLOAD_MANAGER_URL,
847:                 router_url=ROUTER_URL,
848:                 session_id=session_id,
849:             )
850:             client.execute_command("echo 'should fail'")
851: 
852: 
853: if __name__ == "__main__":
854:     # Add verbose flag if not present
855:     if "--verbose" not in sys.argv:
856:         sys.argv.append("--verbose")
857:     unittest.main()
858: ```

937: ```bash
938: #!/usr/bin/env bash
939: set -e
940: 
941: # Source kube_codegen functions
942: source "$(dirname "$0")/kube_codegen.sh"
943: 
944: # Generate client code for runtime API
945: kube::codegen::gen_client \
946:     --with-watch \
947:     --output-dir client-go \
948:     --output-pkg github.com/volcano-sh/agentcube/client-go \
949:     --boilerplate hack/boilerplate.go.txt \
950:     --one-input-api runtime/v1alpha1 \
951:     ./pkg/apis
952: 
953: # Run go mod tidy
954: go mod tidy
955: 
956: # Post-process lister files to add GroupResource() method
957: find client-go -name "*lister.go" -exec sed -i '/^type .*Lister interface/i\
958: // GroupResource returns the GroupResource for this resource.\
959: func (l *<TYPE>Lister) GroupResource() schema.GroupResource {\
960:     return schema.GroupResource{Group: "<GROUP>", Resource: "<RESOURCE>"}\
961: }
962: ' {} \;
963: 
964: echo "Code generation complete"
965: ```

968: ```bash
969: #!/usr/bin/env bash
970: set -e
971: 
972: BOILERPLATE_GO="hack/boilerplate.go.txt"
973: BOILERPLATE_PY="hack/boilerplate.py.txt"
974: 
975: # Function to add copyright header if missing
976: add_copyright() {
977:     local file="$1"
978:     local boilerplate="$2"
979:     
980:     if ! head -n 10 "$file" | grep -q "Copyright The Volcano Authors"; then
981:         echo "Adding copyright to $file"
982:         cat "$boilerplate" "$file" | sponge "$file"
983:     fi
984: }
985: 
986: # Process Go files
987: export -f add_copyright
988: find . -name "*.go" -not -path "./vendor/*" -not -path "./.git/*" | \
989:     while read -r file; do
990:         add_copyright "$file" "$BOILERPLATE_GO"
991:     done
992: 
993: # Process Python files
994: find . -name "*.py" -not -path "./venv/*" -not -path "./.git/*" | \
995:     while read -r file; do
996:         add_copyright "$file" "$BOILERPLATE_PY"
997:     done
998: 
999: echo "Copyright headers updated"
1000: ```

15: |-------|-----------|
16: | Language (server) | Go 1.24, module `github.com/volcano-sh/agentcube` |
17: | Language (CLI/SDK) | Python ≥3.10 |
18: | HTTP framework | Gin (Go), Typer (Python CLI), FastAPI (example) |
19: | Kubernetes | CRDs (`runtime.agentcube.volcano.sh/v1alpha1`), controller-runtime, `sigs.k8s.io/agent-sandbox` |

25: ## Architecture

28: Client / SDK / CLI
29:   → Router (Deployment :8080)
30:      ↔ Redis/Valkey (session store)
31:      → WorkloadManager (Service :8080)
32:         ↔ Redis/Valkey
